project: Phase 7 Protocols, Benchmarks, and Research Papers Seed
description: Extend Phase 7 README content seeding into Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers with a high-confidence curated slice of canonical protocols, evaluation suites, and multi-agent research papers, and update docs/taxonomy.md to reflect completed versus deferred seeding scope.

context.customerAsk: Phase 7 initial content expansion: populate Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers with the next high-confidence curated README slice. Add roughly 3-5 stable entries to Protocols and Interfaces, 3-5 stable entries to Benchmarks, and 5-8 stable entries to Research Papers using exact resource names as link text, concise factual descriptions ending in periods, and alphabetical order within each section. Prioritize canonical resources that directly enable or evaluate multi-agent coordination, agent-to-agent contracts, handoffs, shared state, workflow orchestration, or group-level agent performance. Update docs/taxonomy.md so its Phase 7 status note reflects that foundational seeding is already present on main for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists, and that this batch extends seeding into Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers. Leave Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates for a later batch. Keep make check, make test, and git diff --check passing.
context.problem: The README has seeded Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists on main, but Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers remain empty. Contributors lack canonical starting points for agent contracts, group-level evaluation harnesses, and coordination research, and docs/taxonomy.md still describes the three target sections as unseeded.
context.solution: Add curated, alphabetized, scope-aligned entries to the three target README sections following existing entry conventions, update the taxonomy Phase 7 status paragraph to document what is seeded on main versus deferred, and verify make check, make test, and git diff --check pass.

acceptanceCriteria: Protocols and Interfaces contains five alphabetized canonical protocol entries with exact link-text names, period-ending factual descriptions, and agent-factory scope keywords
Benchmarks contains five alphabetized canonical benchmark entries evaluating group or workflow behavior with valid entry format and no duplicate URLs or names versus other sections
Research Papers contains seven alphabetized canonical papers on multi-agent coordination, orchestration, or societies with arXiv or equivalent research URLs distinct from Frameworks entries

docs/taxonomy.md Phase 7 status states foundational seeding is on main for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists, that this batch seeds Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers, and that Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates remain for a later batch
Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates remain without resource entries; no new README sections or unrelated governance changes are introduced
Quality gate: make check, make test, and git diff --check all exit 0 from repository root; Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed Protocols and Interfaces section
userStories.description: As a contributor designing multi-agent handoffs, I want the Protocols and Interfaces section to list canonical interaction contracts so I can find interoperable standards without guessing category fit.
acceptanceCriteria: ## Protocols and Interfaces contains exactly five entries in alphabetical order: Agent2Agent Protocol, AGNTCY Agent Connect Protocol, FIPA Agent Communication Language, Model Context Protocol, and Open Agent Schema Framework with canonical URLs and scope-aligned descriptions ending in periods
Each entry uses format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. and passes make check scope-keyword and marketing-phrase rules
Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates gain no new entries
make check exits 0 after the README update
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed Benchmarks section
userStories.description: As a maintainer evaluating agent factories, I want the Benchmarks section to list canonical group- and workflow-oriented evaluation suites so readers can measure coordination and multi-step flow behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: ## Benchmarks contains exactly five entries in alphabetical order: AgentBench, AgentBoard, MultiAgentBench, SWE-bench, and WebArena with canonical URLs and scope-aligned descriptions ending in periods
No duplicate URLs or resource names (case-insensitive) with entries in other README sections
make check exits 0 after the README update
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed Research Papers section
userStories.description: As a researcher or practitioner, I want the Research Papers section to list foundational multi-agent coordination literature so the list anchors academic context for agent-factory design.
acceptanceCriteria: ## Research Papers contains exactly seven entries in alphabetical order: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents, AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation, CAMEL: Communicative Agents for Mind Exploration of Large Language Model Society, Communicative Agents for Software Development, Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior, Large Language Model based Multi-Agents: A Survey of Progress and Challenges, and MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
Paper entries link to arXiv or equivalent research URLs, not duplicate framework homepages already listed under Frameworks
Descriptions are factual, end with periods, and include agent-factory scope keywords
make check exits 0 after the README update
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update taxonomy Phase 7 seeding status
userStories.description: As a planner or maintainer reading taxonomy guidance, I want the Phase 7 status note to accurately describe which README sections are seeded on main and which remain for later batches.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/taxonomy.md Phase 7 status paragraph states foundational seeding is present on main for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists
The same paragraph states this batch extends seeding into Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, and Research Papers
The paragraph explicitly defers Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates to a later batch
No contradictory claims remain that Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, or Research Papers are empty after this batch lands
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify repository quality gate
userStories.description: As a maintainer merging Phase 7 content, I want end-to-end verification that the seeded README and taxonomy update pass all automated checks without whitespace regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, make test exits 0
From repository root, git diff --check exits 0
README Contents anchor list still matches section headings; Blog Posts, Case Studies, and Examples and Templates remain without resource entries
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
